title: heteropterys fulva
intro: Heteropterys fulva, also known as the "Santa Maria" plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It is native to the Amazon rainforest and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimalarial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in the treatment of certain types of cancer. Heteropterys fulva is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and may also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
